tree.label: Abscesses and boils
tree.gujarati: Boils are usually small areas (penny or nickel size) with a thin covering of skin, while abscesses are larger raised areas on the skin that are tender to touch and filled with pus in the deeper tissue.\r\nAbscesses and boils may drain when the skin over the infected area opens and lets the fluid or pus out.\r\nAn abscess under your skin is easy to see. It may appear red, raised and swollen. The skin over the centre of the abscess may be thin. It may look yellow or white because there’s pus underneath the surface of your skin. The abscess may feel tender and warm to the touch. Other symptoms of a skin abscess include pain, fever and chills.

tree.label: Acne
tree.gujarati: Acne is a skin condition that occurs when your hair follicles become plugged with oil and dead skin cells. It causes whiteheads, blackheads or pimples

tree.label: Allergies Seasonal
tree.gujarati: An itch is an unpleasant tingly sensation that makes you want to scratch. If you’re allergic to poison ivy and you go hiking through a field of it, you’ll have a terrible itch all over your legs.\r\n\r\nMany people find relief with self-care measures such as moisturizing daily, using gentle cleansers and bathing with lukewarm water. Long-term relief requires identifying and treating the cause of itchy skin.

tree.label: Anxiety and shock
tree.gujarati: A person has emotional distress or sudden fright, their body releases adrenaline into the bloodstream, but this usually reverses itself in a healthy person. This is where the confusion in the term ‘shock’ sometimes occurs. This ‘non-medical shock’ is a response to anxiety or fear.

tree.label: Backache
tree.gujarati: Backache is one of the most common medical complaints, particularly among adults. Backache usually refers to pain in your muscles, intervertebral joints, spinal nerves, or sometimes bone-on-bone pain. The type of pain you are having can help your physician pinpoint the cause.\r\n\r\nBackache can be categorized as acute or chronic. Acute pain is often temporary and can improve on its own, frequently without treatment or with the assistance of a mild pain relieving medication. Acute backache usually lasts anywhere from a couple of days to several weeks. Chronic back pain is more long-term and can extend beyond several months. The pain can often be progressive, getting worse over time.

tree.label: Bedwetting
tree.gujarati: Bed-Wetting is a phenomenon in which a child urinates, involuntarily, on bed when fast asleep at night.\r\n\r\nGenerally, bedwetting before age 7 isn’t a concern. At this age, your child may still be developing night time bladder control.

tree.label: Grief
tree.gujarati:
Grief is the experience of losing someone important to us. It is characterised by grief, which is the process and the range of emotions we go through as we gradually adjust to the loss.

Losing someone important to us can be emotionally devastating – whether that be a partner, family member, friend or pet. It is natural to go through a range of physical and emotional processes as we gradually come to terms with the loss.
